sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes
Requested: Treasury withdrawal to fund Tweag by Modus Create's delivery of three Cardano core infrastructure work packages over 2026-2027, administered by Intersect.
Recipient: Intersect 2025 treasury reserve contract stake address stake17xzc8pt7fgf0lc0x7eq6z7z6puhsxmzktna7dluahrj6g6ghh5qjr, with Tweag by Modus Create as delivery entity.

Deliverables: Peras v1 mainnet readiness including production cryptography, KillSwitch, hard-fork readiness, support, and maintenance, History Expiry partial-history node work intended to reduce SPO storage costs, Conformance Testing framework extensions for Peras and Leios, Ready-for-activation deliverables including merged code, reproducible releases, runbooks, benchmarks, and governance-action packages, Public demos at least every two months, regular website status updates, Discord engagement, and Slack coordination
Deadline/expiry: 2026-2027; exact milestone dates not stated in document
Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal requests ₳18,263,496.00, stated as USD $4,565,874.00, for three Cardano infrastructure work packages delivered over 2026-2027.
Claim (technical, proposer_asserted, high materiality): Peras v1 is presented as enabling faster finality of about 2 minutes versus about 12 minutes today.

Claim (technical, proposer_asserted, high materiality): History Expiry is presented as necessary because Leios-driven throughput could cause SPO disk usage to surge to about 1 GB per hour at 100-1000 TPS, making full-history requirements economically prohibitive.
Claim (technical, proposer_asserted, medium materiality): The three work packages are described as interdependent parts of a single delivery pipeline rather than separable modules.

Claim (economic, proposer_asserted, high materiality): The budget is said to be based on a $176/hour average market rate for senior Cardano infrastructure engineers and a conservative ADA/USD conversion rate of 0.25 based on a five-year average.

Claim (adoption, proposer_asserted, medium materiality): Tweag states it has worked on Cardano core infrastructure since January 2018, including leading consensus and ledger teams, implementing Ouroboros Genesis, and contributing to Peras design.
missing: Independent evidence for: Peras v1 is presented as enabling faster finality of about 2 minutes versus about 12 minutes today.

Independent evidence for: History Expiry is presented as necessary because Leios-driven throughput could cause SPO disk usage to surge to about 1 GB per hour at 100-1000 TPS, making full-history requirements economically prohibitive.
Independent evidence for: The three work packages are described as interdependent parts of a single delivery pipeline rather than separable modules.

Independent evidence for: The budget is said to be based on a $176/hour average market rate for senior Cardano infrastructure engineers and a conservative ADA/USD conversion rate of 0.25 based on a five-year average.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Treasury analysis
findings: Six-month treasury flow regime: unknown (stale snapshot)
sections.conclusion: Treasury votes require a higher bar because they consume shared ADA and create precedent.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "The proposal requests ₳18,263,496.00, stated as USD $4,565,874.00, for three Cardano infrastructure work packages delivered over 2026-2027." and clears the evidence gates.
Strongest NO: a material claim is unsupported — "Peras v1 is presented as enabling faster finality of about 2 minutes versus about 12 minutes today." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit.
Strongest hold: a treasury action without a complete deep-research dossier cannot be voted directionally without pretending certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.
